summary: Estimated Prophet > Eyes spark, tight jams and stellar second set
blurb: The show erupts after the Estimated Prophet → Eyes transition, delivering a hot second‑set arc that includes a searing Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain and a tight Lost Sailor/Althea pairing. Jerry’s guitar shines despite laryngitis, and Brent adds colorful synth textures. Highlights also feature Franklin’s Tower and Johnny B. Goode.
review: August 5, 1979 at Oakland Auditorium Arena captures the Dead in prime form. After an unremarkable first set, the band lights a fire with the Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World sequence, rolling straight into a psychedelic blaze of Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain. The second set stays hot through Not Fade Away, Wharf Rat and Around & Around, while the encore‑closing Johnny B. Goode leaves the audience buzzing. Jerry Garcia is “feeling it” – his guitar tone is crisp and his voice surprisingly strong despite lingering laryngitis – and Phil Lesh provides a solid, sometimes sweet bass foundation (notably during the Eyes jam). Brent Mydland’s synths and vocal harmonies add that distinctive late‑‘70s texture without ever getting in the way. Overall the performance feels locked‑in, with standout moments that make this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across four of five recordings (praise for Estimated Prophet/Eyes, Scarlet>Fire, Lost/Althea, and Jerry's playing) outweighs the single mixed review that notes only recording glitches. No consistent negative comments about musical performance were found.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard source with clear mix; captures the full set despite a few song cuts, and is consistently praised as the most complete representation of the performance.

key_highlights: Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World sequence ignites the second set.
Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain delivers a psychedelic fire‑storm.
Lost Sailor into Althea pairing showcases tight interplay and vocal harmonies.
Johnny B. Goode encore caps the night with high‑energy excitement.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is on fire – his guitar tone shines and his voice stays strong despite laryngitis, driving the band with confident leads.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh comes in strong, laying down a solid bass foundation; his solo during Eyes Of The World is noted as sweet and standout.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland adds colorful synth textures and vocal harmonies that enhance the late‑'70s sound without overwhelming the mix.
